warnings: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose: Fukui function (reactivity indices) for furan were calculated using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard'). The molecule was considered in the gas phase with charge 0 and multiplicity 1. The input geometry originated from a file at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/furan.xyz`. The computation returned Fukui indices (f+ for electrophilicity and f− for nucleophilicity) for each atom in the molecule. Notably, the most electrophilic site was found on the carbon atom at position 5 (atom index 4, f+ = 1.9242931), while the same site was also the most nucleophilic (f- = 0.1616371). No convergence issues were noted, and warnings related to charge conservation (f+ and f-) were present, indicating sums of f values precisely equaling ±1.0000, which caused no deviations. Live output results were logged for transparency.
